# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { openKv } from 'https://deno.land/x/deno_kv/mod.ts';

const kv = openKv('edge-database');

// KV keys are constructed like ['users', userId, filterParam]
async function getUserData(userId: string, filterParam: string) {
  const key = ['users', userId, filterParam].join(':');
  const data = await kv.get(key);
  return data;
}

// Search functionality
async function searchUserData(query: string) {
  const keys = await kv.keys();
  const results = keys
    .filter((key) => key.includes(query))
    .map(async (key) => ({
      key,
      value: await kv.get(key),
    }));

  return await Promise.all(results);
}

// Deno Deploy API
addEventListener('fetch', (event) => {
  const url = new URL(event.request.url);
  const userId = url.searchParams.get('userId');
  const filterParam = url.searchParams.get('filterParam');
  const searchQuery = url.searchParams.get('search');

  if (searchQuery) {
    event.respondWith(
      searchUserData(searchQuery).then((results) =>
        new Response(JSON.stringify(results), {
          status: 200,
          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        })
      )
    );
  } else if (userId && filterParam) {
    event.respondWith(
      getUserData(userId, filterParam).then((data) =>
        new Response(JSON.stringify(data), {
          status: 200,
          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        })
      )
    );
  } else {
    event.respondWith(
      new Response('Invalid request', { status: 400 })
    );
  }
});
